horizon: Season of the First Wish — the bridge stands and the fair is open; the town pays what it promised, in order, and the far doorsteps get their turn to be stories
goals: The ledger leaves Winsome's head: propose the wish-ledger schema through the gate (needs-ep-review). The book now holds one wish, one pledge paid, and three standing, and it is still stored nowhere but prose — no issue even exists yet; open one

The fair pays in the pledged order, unhurried: Humble Pot's measured bowls next, then Granny Ash's slipper afternoon with the whole town guessing the foot, and Tam's match on the new stone dead last — it is the season's ending, and endings are not rushed into one day. Each redemption earns its Chronicle line and its rumor the same day; the count stands at zero, keep it there

When Tam's match is struck, the first wish is paid: close the bridge's debt in the well's book the same night, at whatever narrative interest it has earned, and let the lane hear the well's first account settled out loud

Eight chunks hold one resident each whose every tie lives across town — Brindle, Bramble Rose, and six more standing on ground that reads as background (Decree 7, issue #165). Give each solitary ground one locally discoverable story: a visit received, a trail that starts there, a debt collected at the door — drawn from ties already on record. No new lore; the material is all there
